prompt: A manga-style JoJo-inspired character card poster set in a narrow urban alley, with dramatic cel shading, heavy ink lines, stylized speed-line energy, and a vertical comic-page layout. The main subject is a young Japanese man, タカ・ハシ, age 19, shown from mid-thigh up in the left foreground, raising his right fist high in a triumphant pose while holding a handheld microphone near his mouth with his left hand. His face is intentionally obscured by a rectangular mosaic blur. He has short messy green hair and wears a fitted green track jacket with gold side stripes and a large golden phoenix emblem across the chest, zipped up, with black pants and black fingerless gloves. To his right and slightly behind him stands his humanoid Stand, サガミ・サヴァイブ (SAGAMI SURVIVE), a compact armored mascot-like figure with a rounded pale yellow bird head, sharp angry eyes, an orange beak, and a sleek white-and-green mechanical body with metallic silver musculature. The Stand has green shoulder armor, green gauntlets, gold trim, and a bold letter S on each side of its chest armor. It is posed punching forward with both fists, aggressive and energetic. Include exactly 4 distinct text panels and effects: 1 large white rectangular description box at the top right filled with dense Japanese text explaining the Stand, 1 jagged white speech bubble at the mid-right with vertical Japanese battle cries, 1 white character-info box at the bottom left with Japanese profile text, and 1 white stat box at the bottom right containing a radar chart labeled STAND PARAMETERS with Japanese and English category labels and a 5-line stat list. Add exactly 4 stylized purple sound-effect texts around the figures: 1 large "ゴゴゴゴ…" near the upper middle, 1 "ド" near the lower left, and 2 separate "ドド" effects near the lower center and lower right. The background is a gray-blue city alley with tall buildings receding in perspective. Overall tone: parody homage to JoJo's Bizarre Adventure character introductions, combining comic intensity with a goofy mascot Stand concept, polished manga poster composition, sharp print-like detail, and Japanese magazine page aesthetics.
